flashcards.back: The maximum limit set by RBI on the aggregate net position (long and short) a bank can hold across all foreign currencies.
RBI mandates a minimum Capital to Risk-weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) of 9% for Indian banks.
A buffer of 2.5% of Risk Weighted Assets (RWA) intended to be used during periods of stress.
Your account with us (e.g., a foreign bank maintaining an INR account with an Indian bank).
Our account with you (e.g., an Indian bank maintaining a USD account with a US bank).
Their account with you (e.g., Bank A referring to Bank B's account with Bank C).
Duration / (1 + Yield). It measures the price sensitivity of a bond to changes in interest rates.
Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) + Additional Tier 1 (AT1). Includes share capital, stock premium, and perpetual non-cumulative preference shares.

questions.question: A bank has Risk Weighted Assets (RWA) of ₹10,000 Crore. As per Basel III (India), what is the minimum total capital (including CCB) it must maintain?
Which of the following risks is NOT part of the Pillar 1 capital requirement under Basel III?
questions.explanation: Pillar 1 covers Credit, Market, and Operational risks. Liquidity risk is managed under Pillar 2 (Internal Capital Adequacy Assessment Process - ICAAP).
